prompts:
/brainstorm_w_team  I have a multi-project orchestration hub (see
  specs/multi-project-orchestration-hub.md for the full spec). The dashboard and
  server are built and working. Now I need the pipeline to actually work end-to-end.
  Here's what's missing:

  1. Task lifecycle via specs/ — Each registered project should track its tasks as
  spec files inside that project's own specs/ folder. When a task moves through
  stages (backlog → brainstorm → plan → implement → test_impact → review → fix →
  done), the spec file itself should have a Stage: field that gets updated. The hub
  server reads these spec files to know what stage each task is at. The spec file is
  the source of truth, not just the database.

  2. Update commands to report stage changes — The existing skills
  (brainstorm_w_team, plan_w_team, implement_w_team, test_impact, review) should each
   call the hub API (PATCH /api/projects/:id/tasks/:taskId) to update the task's
  stage when they start and finish. For example, when plan_w_team stage to plan; when it finishes, it advances to the next stage. This way the Kanban
   board updates in real-time.

  3. Adding tasks to backlog — I need a way to add a new task. Options: (a) a new
  add_task command/skill that takes a plain-language description, creates a spec file
   in the project's specs/ folder with Stage: backlog, and registers it via POST
  /api/projects/:id/tasks, or (b) through the dashboard registration page, or (c)
  just by telling Claude "add a task to [project]" and having the CLAUDE.md routing
  handle it. I want option (a) + (c).

  4. Clean up test data — Remove the test projects (event-test, task-test,
  test-project) from the database so only VideoDublication remains.

  The goal: I say "add a task to VideoDublication: build user authentication" and it
  creates a spec, registers it on the hub as backlog, and I can see it on the Kanban
  board. Then when I say "work on that task", it flows through brainstorm → plan →
  implement → etc., with each stage updatingard in real-time.
\
\
